current_focus: Treat K=128 only as the initial search anchor, not as a success criterion. The research objective is to find configurations whose FVU is dramatically better than the current K=128 baseline, with a target on the order of halving that baseline FVU before rewarding smaller K or lower cost.

recent_results.description: Hold the improved JumpReLU K=128 setup fixed at LR=1.6e-3 and start the auxk sweep by setting AUXK_ALPHA=0 to test whether pure reconstruction training beats the current anchor.
Keep the best-quality JumpReLU K=128 configuration fixed at LR=1.6e-3 and continue the auxk sweep with AUXK_ALPHA=0.01 after the zero-auxk ablation underperformed.

Keep the best K=128 JumpReLU quality anchor fixed and begin the expansion-factor sweep with EXPANSION_FACTOR=16, since the recent auxk sweep underperformed and capacity is the next clean one-parameter probe.

recent_round_summaries.hypothesis: JumpReLU at K=128 may be paying unnecessary reconstruction cost to the auxiliary dead-feature objective, and removing auxk pressure could lower primary FVU below the current 0.1724 full frontier.

JumpReLU at K=128 may still benefit from a reduced but nonzero auxiliary dead-feature objective, and lowering AUXK_ALPHA from 0.03125 to 0.01 could preserve feature health while reducing the reconstruction penalty seen at the baseline auxk weight.

JumpReLU at K=128 appears limited more by encoder capacity than by auxk tuning, so increasing expansion_factor from 8 to 16 while holding the improved LR=1.6e-3 and AUXK_ALPHA=0.03125 fixed may reduce proxy FVU below the current JumpReLU proxy anchor.

gated.design_hypothesis: A gated SAE that decouples latent support selection from reconstruction magnitude can beat the current JumpReLU anchor at K=128 by learning a separate sigmoid routing branch while staying initialization-close to plain ReLU top-k.
next_steps: If gated shows better proxy FVU at acceptable throughput, stabilize the family by exposing and tuning gate temperature/init strength in a follow-up.
If gated quality is promising but throughput regresses, keep the family alive and do a performance-focused follow-up rather than discarding the architecture.
If gated fails to improve proxy FVU, move on to a different routing-focused family instead of more JumpReLU-local sweeps.

recent_performance_findings: round 3: suspected performance regression for jumprelu (tps=16601.650675, baseline_ratio=0.200000, termination=throughput_too_low)
next_move_guidance: If EXPANSION_FACTOR=16 improves proxy FVU, promote to full and then decide whether to stabilize EF further or tune LR on the higher-capacity model.
If EXPANSION_FACTOR=16 is worse or neutral, test EXPANSION_FACTOR=4 to map whether the current EF=8 is already near-optimal.
If both EF=16 and EF=4 fail, conclude local JumpReLU capacity sweeps are exhausted and return to architecture exploration or a fresh optimizer-level change.
